description: Manifest-bound schedule-aware analysis for Run 029 longer two-transmitter microSD-backed physical replay.

b.role: medium usefulness-threshold 64-row schedule replayed from microSD

expected_headline.txa: TXA/N01: 64/64 schedule rows SEND; 478 received packets; mean delivered usefulness 0.526
expected_headline.txb: TXB/N16: 32/64 schedule rows SEND; 241 received packets; mean delivered usefulness 0.810
expected_headline.ratio: Observed received-packet ratio 0.5042; scheduled send-fraction ratio 0.5000.

interpretation: Run 029 extends microSD-backed scheduled replay from a 16-row schedule to a 64-row two-transmitter schedule. TXA/N01 loaded a fixed-all 64/64 SEND schedule and TXB/N16 loaded a medium usefulness-threshold 32/64 SEND schedule from /schedule.csv. The observed TXB/TXA received-packet ratio was close to the scheduled send-fraction ratio, while TXB retained higher mean delivered usefulness per received packet than TXA. This supports a bounded longer-schedule SD replay interpretation under similar two-transmitter lab conditions.
cautions: This is point-to-point LoRa at 915 MHz, not LoRaWAN.
The schedule CSVs define one repeated schedule period.
The analyzer compares schedule proportions and observed packet proportions.
The analyzer does not infer exact transmitted packet counts.
Missing sequence numbers are observed sequence gaps, not confirmed collisions.
recv_ms and tx_ms are not synchronized across boards and should not be interpreted as true latency.
Receiver inter-arrival summaries are receiver-side observations only.
Usefulness and priority are synthetic metadata in this milestone.
The run does not yet use a live belief-maintenance controller.
Use the wording reduced physical transmission attempts under scheduled skipping, not airtime optimization.
Do not claim energy savings unless current or power measurements are added.
Run 029 changes schedule length relative to Run 028, not transmitter count.
